title: Vitis miqueliana
taxonomy: Vitis miqueliana is a species of grapevine in the family Vitaceae. It is native to East Asia and is a part of the genus Vitis, which includes over 60 species of grapevines. The classification of Vitis miqueliana places it within the subgenus Euvitis, which is a group of grapevines that are primarily native to Asia. Within the subgenus, Vitis miqueliana is often classified as part of the series Miquelianae, which includes several species of grapevines found in East Asia. This classification system is based on the plant's morphology, distribution, and genetic characteristics.

constituents: Vitis miqueliana, also known as the Japanese wild grape, is a species of grapevine. The active constituents of Vitis miqueliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds. These compounds have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. intro: Vitis miqueliana, also known as the Taiwan wild grape, is a species of grapevine native to Taiwan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to exhibit various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties.
